title: podman stats - not showing anything
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have two containers running, but when executing (sudo) podman stats it is not showing any statistics at all.

**Describe the results you received:**

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20441822/90267610-1a6b1300-de56-11ea-8396-c54d70454ee9.png)

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/20441822/90267692-37074b00-de56-11ea-8d56-7ff8cb604e73.png)

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would expect to see statistics about CPU and memory usage.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

n/a 

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.0.2
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.4
Git Commit:   201c9505b88f451ca877d29a73ed0f1836bb96c7
Built:        Sun Jul 12 22:46:58 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
It looks like your containers are running rootless (no sudo). Have you tried running `podman stats` without sudo as well?

`sudo podman stats` would show containers that were run with `sudo podman`

I believe that we expect 'podman stats' to not work as rootless on cgroups
v1 sirens (it requires cgroups to identify what processes are in the
container and their resource usage, AFAIK - but it's been a while since I
was in that part of the code). Suspicion: the part of the code that should
have detected an unsupported configuration for stats and errored was
accidentally dropped in the migration to v2.0.

On Sat, Aug 15, 2020, 09:01 Daniel Lamando <notifications@github.com> wrote:

> It looks like your containers are running rootless (no sudo). Have you
> tried running podman stats without sudo as well?
>
> sudo podman stats would show containers that were run with sudo podman
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you are subscribed to this thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7325#issuecomment-674393821>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AB3AOCDUFFFMJSXSVK34A6LSA2BIRANCNFSM4P7UB2BQ>
> .
>
@mheon I still get the error `Error: stats is not supported in rootless mode without cgroups v2` in podman v2 with cgroup v1.

@mheon: It's exactly as @Luap99 wrote: `Error: stats is not supported in rootless mode without cgroups v2`. However I would expect that with sudo (since it has bigger privileges) it would display those stats even if containers are running without sudo.

`sudo podman` only knows about containers created by root, so it cannot show you the stats for rootless containers. If you want to see stats on rootless with cgroups v1 you can use regular `top` or `htop` to see the resources of all processes including the ones in your containers.

Or you migrate to cgroups v2 but this requires to recreate all containers AFAIK.

I have tried to use cgroups v2 but since I'm kind of Linux newbie and I got many strange errors I just recreated all containers with sudo.

Thanks for help guys!
